2606.11930.title: Frozen Multimodal Embeddings for Personality and Cognitive Ability Assessment in Asynchronous Video Interviews
2606.11930.paper_summary: Predicting psychological traits from asynchronous video interviews (AVIs) is a challenging multimodal learning problem because labeled datasets are limited while each response contains high-dimensional visual, acoustic, and verbal signals. This paper presents our solution for the ACM Multimedia AVI Challenge 2026, which evaluates two tasks: Track~1 predicts self-reported HEXACO personality traits from personality-related interview responses, and Track~2 classifies cognitive ability levels from structured AVI responses. We treat the problem as a small-sample representation learning task. Instead of fine-tuning large pretrained models, we use frozen multimodal encoders, including CLIP for visual features, Whisper for acoustic features and transcripts, and RoBERTa, E5, and DeBERTaV3 for textual representations, followed by low-capacity downstream models. For Track~1, our trait-specific regression and late-fusion system achieves an average validation MSE of 0.2696, improving over the official baseline of 0.3334. Ablation results show a three-step improvement from a global model (0.3189), to per-trait modeling (0.2871), to per-trait late fusion (0.2696), corresponding to a 19.1\% relative MSE reduction over the official baseline. For Track~2, a compact subject-attribute baseline reaches 0.5781 accuracy, while our multimodal ensemble reaches 0.5313, both above the official baseline of 0.4062. We interpret this result as evidence of possible subject-attribute shortcuts in the validation split rather than robust cognitive inference from AVI content. Overall, our findings suggest that AVI-based psychological assessment benefits from trait-specific multimodal modeling, but cognitive ability prediction requires careful control of dataset shortcuts.
2606.11930.paper_summary_zh: 預測非同步視頻面試（AVI）中的心理特徵是一個具有挑戰性的多模態學習問題，因為標記數據集有限，而每個回應都包含高維的視覺、聲音和語言信號。本文提出了我們對2026年ACM多媒體AVI挑戰賽的解決方案，該挑戰評估兩個任務：Track~1預測從與人格相關的面試回應中自我報告的HEXACO人格特徵，而Track~2則從結構化的AVI回應中分類認知能力水平。我們將此問題視為小樣本表示學習任務。我們不對大型預訓練模型進行微調，而是使用凍結的多模態編碼器，包括用於視覺特徵的CLIP、用於聲音特徵和轉錄的Whisper，以及用於文本表示的RoBERTa、E5和DeBERTaV3，然後使用低容量的下游模型。對於Track~1，我們的特徵特定回歸和後期融合系統達到了0.2696的平均驗證均方誤差（MSE），優於官方基線0.3334。消融結果顯示，從全局模型（0.3189）到每個特徵建模（0.2871），再到每個特徵的後期融合（0.2696），經歷了三步改進，這對應於相對於官方基線的19.1\% MSE減少。對於Track~2，一個緊湊的主體-屬性基線達到了0.5781的準確率，而我們的多模態集成達到了0.5313，兩者均高於官方基線0.4062。我們將這一結果解釋為驗證分割中可能存在的主體-屬性捷徑的證據，而非從AVI內容中進行的穩健認知推斷。總的來說，我們的研究結果表明，基於AVI的心理評估受益於特徵特定的多模態建模，但認知能力預測需要對數據集捷徑進行仔細控制。
2606.11930.author: Kuo-En Hung et.al.
2606.11930.authors: Kuo-En Hung, Hung-Yue Suen, Shih-Ching Yeh, Hsiang-Wen Wang
